settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Appearance, language, and connection preferences.
settings.daemonExternal: The daemon is managed externally (e.g. a systemd user service), so this app has nothing to stop. Use `systemctl --user stop connectibled`.
settings.appearanceHint: Pick a monochrome theme. All themes are dark by design.
settings.languageHint: Language used across the whole app.
settings.daemonHint: Background service handling device discovery, pairing, and data sync.
settings.filesHint: Where files sent to this device are saved.
home.notPairedHint: Open Connectible on another device nearby, and it will appear here to pair.
manual.title: Connect by address
manual.subtitle: No auto-discovery needed. Type the address shown on the other device.
manual.invalid: Enter a valid IP address and port (1-65535).
devices.emptyHint: Make sure another device on this network is running Connectible. It will show up here automatically.
menu.forgetConfirm: Forget this device? You'll need to pair it again to reconnect.
clipboard.emptyHint: Copy text on this or a paired device and it appears here, synced instantly.
transfers.selectTarget: Select a reachable device to send to.
transfers.dropHint: Drag files onto this window to send them to the selected device.
transfers.dropNoTarget: Pair a device first to send files.
transfers.emptyHint: Send a file to a paired device, or receive one - progress shows up here live.
input.acceptDesc: Lets a paired phone control this computer's mouse and keyboard over the encrypted stream.
input.noBackendPre: No input backend detected. On Linux this needs a running
input.noBackendPost: (X11) or a wlroots-based Wayland compositor. See the operator docs to enable it.
input.waitingHint: Mouse and keyboard events from a paired device will be applied here in real time.
input.toggleHint: Turn off to ignore incoming input events without disconnecting anything.
notifications.emptyHint: Notifications from your paired phone will mirror here. Dismissing on either device clears it on both.
pairing.wantsToPair: {name} wants to pair. Enter this code on that device.
pairing.timedOut: Pairing timed out. Start again to retry.
pairing.incorrectPin: Incorrect PIN. Check the code on the other device and try again.
pairing.pairedSub: You're connected and ready to sync.
pairingQr.title: Pair a phone
pairingQr.subtitle: Open Connectible on your phone and scan this code to pair -- no typing needed.
pairingQr.noAddress: No network address found. Connect this computer to a network and try again.
pairingQr.renderFailed: Couldn't render the code. Try again.
settings.clipboardSyncHint: Share clipboard content between paired devices automatically.
settings.clipboardSyncToggleHint: Turn off to stop sharing clipboard changes with paired devices.
settings.pairingQrHint: Show a QR code your phone can scan to pair instantly.
doctor.title: System Doctor
doctor.checks.diskSpace: Free disk space for incoming files
doctor.msg.diskSpace.low.remediation: Free up space on the download volume or point the download directory at one with more room.
doctor.msg.dbKeySource.fallbackFile.warn: Using the local fallback key file, not the OS keyring
doctor.msg.dbKeySource.fallbackFile.reme: Expected for a headless systemd service with no session bus. On a desktop session, check that a Secret Service provider (GNOME Keyring, KWallet, ...) is running if you expected keyring-backed storage instead.
doctor.msg.dbKeySource.unavailable.remed: Check permissions on the TLS directory (fallback key file location) and, if using CONNECTIBLE_DB_KEY_FILE, that the path is valid.
doctor.msg.dirWritable.missingUncreatabl: Check the parent path exists and the daemon has permission.
doctor.msg.dirWritable.notWritable.remed: Fix the directory's ownership/permissions so the daemon can write it.
doctor.msg.lanAddress.none.remediation: Connect this device to the same Wi-Fi/LAN as its peers.
doctor.msg.daemonPort.unreachable.remedi: Start the daemon (connectibled), or check that no firewall blocks the port.
doctor.msg.tlsCert.missing.remediation: Start the daemon once -- it generates a self-signed cert/key on first run.
doctor.msg.pairedStore.dbUnreadable.reme: Check the file's permissions; if corrupt, re-pairing rebuilds it.
doctor.msg.pairedStore.tableUnreadable.r: The database may be from an incompatible version; re-pairing rebuilds it.
doctor.msg.pairedStore.notPinned.remedia: These were paired before certificate pinning; they pin automatically on the next connect.
doctor.msg.displayServer.none.remediatio: Clipboard and remote-input need a graphical session; run the daemon inside your desktop session.
doctor.msg.fileOpener.none.remediation: Install xdg-utils (provides xdg-open) so 'Open' works for received files.
doctor.msg.inputBackend.missing.remediat: Install ydotool and ensure ydotoold is running for remote input on Wayland.
doctor.msg.stuckPartials.some.remediatio: These let interrupted transfers resume; delete them to reclaim space if unwanted.
errors.unspecified: Something went wrong. Try again, and check the daemon logs if it persists.
errors.unauthenticated: This device isn't paired yet. Start pairing again.
errors.pairingRejected: Pairing was rejected. Confirm the PIN on both devices and try again.
errors.pairingTimeout: The pairing code expired. Start pairing again from the beginning.
errors.deviceNotFound: That device is no longer available. Refresh the device list.
errors.fileTransferFailed: The file transfer failed. Retry the transfer.
errors.checksumMismatch: The received file didn't match -- it may be corrupted. Retry the transfer.
errors.unsupportedPlatform: This feature isn't supported on that device.
errors.internal: The daemon hit an internal error. Try again, and check the daemon logs if it persists.
errors.protocolVersionMismatch: These two apps are running incompatible versions. Update both to the same version.
errors.rateLimited: Too many attempts -- wait a moment before retrying.
errors.fingerprintChanged: This device's security key changed since you paired. If you didn't reinstall Connectible on it, this could be an imposter. Forget and re-pair the device to reconnect.
